name: Yelmo de la Pedriza y el Tolmo — circular desde Canto Cochino
region_intro: Jogging routes around Manzanares El Real offer diverse natural landscapes at the foot of the Sierra de Guadarrama, including the unique granite rock formations of La Pedriza. The region features varied terrain, from dirt and stone paths with elevation changes to sandy cattle tracks and technical granite sections. The area is also home to the Santillana Reservoir and the Manzanares River, providing scenic riverside and lakeside running opportunities. Best jogging routes around Manzanares El Real The most popular jogging route is Yelmo de la Pedriza y el Tolmo — circular desde Canto Cochino, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete, navigating through the distinctive granite landscape of La Pedriza. Another top favourite among local runners is Green Puddle of La Pedriza – trail run from Manzanares el Real, a difficult 9.0 miles (14.4 km) path. This trail offers a challenging run through the natural beauty of La Pedriza. Local runners also love the La Pedriza Loop, a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail leading through the iconic granite formations of La Pedriza, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Jogging around Manzanares El Real is defined by the granite rock formations of La Pedriza, the tranquil Santillana Reservoir, and the scenic Manzanares River. The network offers options for various ability levels, from shorter, easier routes to challenging mountain trails with significant elevation changes. The routes in Manzanares El Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore Manzanares El Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Manzanares El Real?
A: Manzanares El Real offers a wide selection of running paths, with over 100 routes available. These range from easier trails to challenging mountain runs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Manzanares El Real?
A: The terrain is quite varied, reflecting the region's diverse landscape. You'll find everything from unique granite rock formations in La Pedriza, requiring technical footwork, to dirt and stone paths with elevation changes, and even sandy cattle tracks for stable, flat strides. The area also features scenic routes around the Santillana Reservoir and along the Manzanares River.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: While many routes in Manzanares El Real are considered challenging, there are options suitable for beginners. The region offers shorter routes of 3 and 5 kilometers adapted for jogging or walking. Additionally, the Urban Running Circuit, approximately 10 kilometers long, provides a mix of historical landmarks and natural areas with varied terrain.

Q: What are some challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the region offers several demanding trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233473617/la-pedriza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedriza Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 10.7-mile (17.2 km) trail through iconic granite formations. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233560965/yelmo-de-la-pedriza-y-el-tolmo-circular-desde-canto-cochino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yelmo de la Pedriza y el Tolmo — circular desde Canto Cochino&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.8-mile (9.3 km) route with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957901235/green-puddle-of-la-pedriza-trail-run-from-manzanares-el-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Puddle of La Pedriza – trail run from Manzanares el Real&lt;/a&gt; also presents a difficult 9.0-mile (14.4 km) path.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Manzanares El Real?
A: Yes, many of the running paths in Manzanares El Real are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233560965/yelmo-de-la-pedriza-y-el-tolmo-circular-desde-canto-cochino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yelmo de la Pedriza y el Tolmo — circular desde Canto Cochino&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233473617/la-pedriza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedriza Loop&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see while running?
A: Manzanares El Real offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can run past the impressive 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2697875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Castle of Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include the unique granite formations of La Pedriza, the tranquil Santillana Reservoir, and the scenic Manzanares River.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Santillana Reservoir?
A: Yes, the surroundings of the Santillana Reservoir offer pleasant running paths with beautiful views of the La Pedriza mountain range. The Urban Running Circuit also traverses natural areas like the surroundings of the Santillana Reservoir, providing opportunities to enjoy this rich ecosystem.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Manzanares El Real?
A: The running routes in Manzanares El Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of La Pedriza, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with historical exploration.

Q: Are there any outdoor fitness areas available for runners?
A: Yes, the Urban Running Circuit in Manzanares El Real is complemented by four outdoor fitness areas. These are equipped with elements like abdominal benches, dip bars, and hurdle jumps, and are located near the football field, Valdelospiés, the Old Castle, and the Cañada de los Toros.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Manzanares El Real?
A: Manzanares El Real is suitable for running year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat, while winter can provide crisp, clear days, though some higher mountain trails might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
A: Absolutely. Manzanares El Real is a key entry point to the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Many routes, especially those venturing into La Pedriza, offer stunning views of the mountainous backdrop, including peaks like Peñalara Glacial Cirque and Seven Peaks.

Q: Can I find routes that are suitable for a longer run, such as a half-marathon distance?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer distances, there are several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233473617/la-pedriza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedriza Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 10.7 miles (17.2 km), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1174137479/manzanares-moralzarzal-and-el-boalo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares, Moralzarzal, and El Boalo Loop&lt;/a&gt; extends to 32.9 km (20.4 miles), offering a significant challenge for longer runs.
tips: It is very rocky and the shapes of the rocks are taken from a fairy tale.
El Tolmo is the starting point to enter the true Pedriza, a large granite batholith located on the southern slope of the Sierra de Guadarrama. It is a wonderful place.
